coll: flyleaf+1x2+1 (4, paper), Quires 1-2 : 2x10 (24), Quire 3 : 1x8 (32), Quire 4 : 1x10 (42), Quires 5-7 : 3x8 (66), Quire 8 : 1x4 (70), Quires 9-14 : 6x8 (118), Quire 15 : 1x6 + 1 (125), Quire 16 : 1x2+1x4 (131); 1x2+pastedown (134, paper)

layer.label: Sinai Greek 468, Undertext (Minuscule bouclée, Greek majuscule)

note: Minuscule bouclée. Titles of hymns in upright pointed majuscule, there are 45 titles in majuscule
Each folio constitutes half of an original folio. The first and last words of text on each folio are missing

note.value: The hymnographical texts are fragments of kanons including troparia-prosomoia
Description by Vasilios Katsaros
Description of undertext by Vasilios Katsaros

note: Combination of early Naskh and Maghribi scripts | Same script as UTO Homily on the Dormition. Large bowls with sweeping curves for a number of descending strokes. Occasionally the stems of the letter alif have a club-like extension to the left of their top point. Many times the loop of letter Ḍād and ṣād has no “tooth”. The base stroke of the initial letter ‘ayn is elongated backward to the right. Sporadic diacritical marks

text_unit.label: Homily on the Rich and the Poor?

work.desc_title: John Chrysostom, Homily on the Rich and the Poor

note.value: The undertext appears to contain a homily on the rich and the poor and seems to refer to the narrative of the rich man and Lazarus in Luke 16:19-31
It may be the case that this homily follows a dormition narrative; compare Sinai Arabic 556, which has a homily by John Chrysostom on the rich and the poor that follows a dormition narrative
Description by Hikmat Kashouh
Reconstructed folio order, ff. 29v, 28r, 29r, 28v
Description of undertext by Hikmat Kashouh

note: Sloping pointed majuscule | Systematic use of punctuation

rel_con.label: John Chrysostom, Saint, -407. | Homilies on Genesis
John Chrysostom, Saint, -407. Homilies on Genesis

note.value: Homily 43, on Genesis 19 (=ΟΜΙΛΙΑ ΜΓ': Ήλθον δε, φησίν, οι δύο άγγελοι εις Σόδομα εσπέρας)
Description by Vasilios Katsaros
Bibliography: J.-P.Migne P.G.53:21-385; 54:385-580
Reconstructed folio order, ff. 130v, 129r, 129v, 130r, 131v, 128r, 128v, 131r
The original manuscript had folios with text in 2 columns. Later, it was cut so that now each folio includes only part of one or the other column
Description of undertext by Vasilios Katsaros
Bibliography: J.-P.Migne P.G.53:21-385; 54:385-580

note: Combination of early Naskh and Maghribi scripts | Same script as UTO Homily on the Rich and the Poor. Large bowls with sweeping curves for a number of descending strokes. Occasionally the stems of letter alif have a club-like extension to the left of their top point, and the loop of letter Ḍād and letter ṣād has no “tooth”. The base stroke of the initial letter ‘ayn is elongated backward to the right. Sporadic diacritical marks

text_unit.label: Homily on the Dormition of the Virgin Mary?

work.desc_title: John of Damascus, Homily on the Dormition of the Virgin Mary

note.value: The undertext appears to contain a homily on, or perhaps the narrative of, the dormition of the Virgin Mary from the Bethlehem tradition
Arabic translator: Abba Antonios or ‘Abdallah ibn al-Fadl al-Antaki?

There is a clear divergence between this undertext object and the well-known published versions (cf. Shoemaker 2002). See also, Graf, Geschichte, Vol. 1, pp. 249-251 and his references to Sinai MSS of similar content. Finally, compare Enger 1854, p. 54 with the undertext on fol. 30r
Description by Hikmat Kashouh

Bibliography: Stephen J. Shoemaker, Ancient Traditions of the Virgin Mary’s Dormition and Assumption. Oxford: University Press, 2002 ; Graf, Geschichte, Vol. 1, 249-251 ; Maximilian Enger, Akhbär Yühanna as-salîh fi-naqlat umm al-Masih, id est Joannis Apostoli de transitu Beatae Mariae Virginis Liber (Eberfeld: R. L. Friderichs, 1854)
Impossible to establish. The following list is based on codicological examination
Description of undertext by Hikmat Kashouh

Bibliography: Stephen J. Shoemaker, Ancient Traditions of the Virgin Mary’s Dormition and Assumption. L. Friderichs, 1854)
